Operation 'userInfo' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_context, missing_output_details. The current description is vague about what is actually returned and lacks clarity about authentication requirements. It focuses on use cases rather than what the operation does. The suggestion clarifies the OIDC UserInfo structure being returned, specifies authentication requirements, and explains when to use this endpoint relative to the Token Endpoint, improving AI agent interpretability.

data.current_description: Use this endpoint to retrieve a user's profile in case you are unable to parse an ID Token or you've not already obtained enough details from the ID Token via the Token Endpoint.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the authenticated user's profile information (UserInfo) as defined by OpenID Connect. Use this endpoint when you cannot parse the ID Token or need additional user claims beyond what was provided in the Token Endpoint response. Returns a UserInfo object containing user identity claims such as name, email, and address. Requires valid access token authentication.
data.reasoning: The current description is vague about what is actually returned and lacks clarity about authentication requirements. It focuses on use cases rather than what the operation does. The suggestion clarifies the OIDC UserInfo structure being returned, specifies authentication requirements, and explains when to use this endpoint relative to the Token Endpoint, improving AI agent interpretability.

message: Operation 'authorizeIframe' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_context, missing_input_details, missing_output_details. The current summary 'Include a session iframe' is vague and action-unclear. The description lacks input parameter context (client_id), output format details (HTML iframe), and clear explanation of what the operation returns. The suggestion clarifies that this is a retrieval operation, specifies the required client_id parameter, explains the response format, and describes the session management capabilities, enabling AI agents to better understand when and how to use this operation.

data.current_description:
An OpenID Connect Session Management iframe to facilitate e.g. single sign-on or remote logouts.

The iframe implements the OIDC postMessage-based [change notification protocol](http://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-session-1_0.html#ChangeNotification) via which a client can receive notifications about session state changes.
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve OpenID Connect session management iframe
data.description_suggestion: Returns an OpenID Connect Session Management iframe that enables session state monitoring and notifications. The iframe implements the OIDC postMessage-based change notification protocol, allowing clients to receive real-time notifications about session state changes (e.g., for single sign-on or remote logouts). Requires the client_id parameter. The response is an HTML iframe element that should be embedded in the client application to maintain session awareness.
data.reasoning: The current summary 'Include a session iframe' is vague and action-unclear. The description lacks input parameter context (client_id), output format details (HTML iframe), and clear explanation of what the operation returns. The suggestion clarifies that this is a retrieval operation, specifies the required client_id parameter, explains the response format, and describes the session management capabilities, enabling AI agents to better understand when and how to use this operation.

data.current_description:
Start a session with Authentiq Connect to authenticate a user.

```
GET https://connect.authentiq.io/authorize?client_id=<your-client-id>&response_type=code+id_token&scope=openid+email&redirect_uri=<your-redirect-uri>&state=0123456789
```

This endpoint also supports the POST method.
data.description_suggestion: Initiates an OAuth 2.0 authorization request to authenticate an end-user. Supports both authorization code and implicit flows with OpenID Connect. Required parameters include client_id, response_type, redirect_uri, and scope. Returns an authorization code or tokens (depending on response_type) via redirect to the specified redirect_uri. The state parameter should be used to prevent CSRF attacks. Supports both GET and POST methods.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks clarity on OAuth 2.0 flow mechanics, required parameters, and return behavior. Missing details about response types, state parameter usage for security, and flow variations. Added specifics about authorization code vs implicit flows, parameter requirements, redirect behavior, and CSRF protection to improve AI agent understanding of when and how to use this operation.

data.current_description:
Register a new client with this Authentiq Connect provider.

This endpoint is compatible with [OIDC's Client Registration](http://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-registration-1_0.html) extension.
data.description_suggestion: Register a new OAuth 2.0 client application with the Authentiq Connect provider. Accepts client configuration parameters (name, redirect_uris, response_types, etc.) in the request body. Compliant with OpenID Connect Client Registration specification. Returns the registered Client object with assigned client_id and client_secret. Requires authentication via client registration token. The created client can then be used for OAuth 2.0 authorization flows.
data.reasoning: Description mentions OIDC compatibility but lacks details about input parameters, authentication requirements, and return values. Missing information about what gets returned (client_id, client_secret) and how the created client is used. Added specifics about request body parameters, authentication method, response contents, and the relationship to OAuth 2.0 flows for improved AI agent understanding.

message: Operation 'clientClient_id' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_context, missing_side_effects. Summary is generic. Description lacks authentication context, side effects (revocation of grants/tokens), and response details. Missing information about what happens to existing tokens and authorizations when a client is deleted. Added authentication requirements, permanent deletion warning, side effects on existing grants/tokens, and HTTP response code for better AI agent understanding of the operation's impact.

data.description_suggestion: Permanently delete a previously registered OAuth 2.0 client application. Requires the client_id path parameter and authentication via client registration token or user JWT. Deleting a client revokes all associated authorization grants and tokens. This operation cannot be undone. Returns HTTP 204 No Content on successful deletion.
data.reasoning: Summary is generic. Description lacks authentication context, side effects (revocation of grants/tokens), and response details. Returns the Client object containing all registered configuration including client_id, redirect_uris, response_types, token_endpoint_auth_method, and other OAuth 2.0 settings.

data.current_description: Update the configuration of a previously registered client.
data.description_suggestion: Update the configuration of a previously registered OAuth 2.0 client. Requires the client_id path parameter and a Client object in the request body with updated configuration fields. Supports partial updates - only provided fields will be modified. Requires authentication via client registration token or user JWT. Returns the updated Client object. Configuration changes take effect immediately for new authorization requests.

data.current_description:
Exchange en authorization code for an ID Token or Access Token.

This endpoint supports both `client_secret_basic` (default) and `client_secret_basic` authentication methods, as specified by the client's `token_endpoint_auth_method`.

- Tags: Authentication
- Parameters: 1 parameter(s)
- Request Schema: inline
data.description_suggestion: Exchange an authorization code for ID Token and/or Access Token as part of the OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow. Required parameters include grant_type (authorization_code), code, client_id, and redirect_uri. Supports client authentication via client_secret_basic (default) or client_secret_post methods as configured in the client's token_endpoint_auth_method. Returns a Token object containing id_token, access_token, token_type, and expires_in. Used to complete the authorization code exchange after user authentication.
data.reasoning: Summary is incomplete (mentions only ID Token, not Access Token). Description has typo ('en' instead of 'an'), mentions duplicate authentication method, and lacks details about required parameters and response structure. Missing information about grant_type, required parameters, and what the Token response contains. Added complete parameter list, clarified authentication methods, specified response contents, and explained the operation's role in the OAuth 2.0 flow for better AI agent understanding.
